nodes.x: Truly God is good to Israel, even to such as are of a clean heart.
O God, thou art terrible out of thy holy places: the God of Israel is he that giveth strength and power unto his people. Blessed be God.
My heart is toward the governors of Israel, that offered themselves willingly among the people. Bless ye the Lord.
Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,
Blessed be the Lord God of Israel for ever and ever. And all the people said, Amen, and praised the Lord.
First I will say, may tbe Lord God of Israel bless you, and comfort your hearts. (Tbe company and bystanders responded AMEN.)
But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.
Thou hast enlarged my steps under me; so that my feet did not slip.

prived me of the privilege. One day upon going down stairs to dinner, I incautiously set my foot upon a round stick, that lay near the top of the stairs. This, rolling under my foot, pitched me forward down the steps; my head was severely bruised in falling; however, I said but little about it, thinking I should be better soon.
Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be healed.
For I was envious at the foolish, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
This is one thing, therefore I said it, He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.
Shall wickedness forever triumph? No! “The haughtiness of man, shall be brought low” —
The crown of the wise is their riches: but the foolishness of fools is folly.
A little that a righteous man hath is better than the riches of many wicked.
For there are no bands in their death: but their strength is firm.
They are raised to dwell with God who has redeemed them; thus they have eternal life through Christ, who has broken the bands of death.

And again I ask, were the bands of death broken, and the chains of hell which encircled them about, were they loosed? I say unto you, Yea, they were loosed, and their souls did expand, and they did sing redeeming love. And I say unto you that they are saved.
But behold, my little band of two thousand and sixty fought most desperately; yea, they were firm before the Lamanites, and did administer death unto all those who opposed them.
They are not in trouble as other men; neither are they plagued like other men.
Men are prone, nevertheless, to attribute worldly misfortunes to wrong doing even though men suffer »n the performance of some God-given requirement.
Therefore pride compasseth them about as a chain; violence covereth them as a garment.
Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.
And again, thou shalt not be proud in thy heart; let all thy garments be plain, and their beauty the beauty of the work of thine own hands;
Every one that is proud shall be thrust through; yea, and every one that is joined to the wicked shall fall by the sword.
O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent men have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.
And cover not their iniquity, and let not their sin be blotted out from before thee: for they have provoked thee to anger before the builders.
Their eyes stand out with fatness: they have more than heart could wish.
The light of the eyes rejoiceth the heart: and a good report maketh the bones fat.
Their heart is as fat as grease; but I delight in thy law.
They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.
Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
They that are of a froward heart are abomination to the Lord: but such as are upright in their way are his delight.
They are corrupt, and speak wickedly concerning oppression: they speak loftily.
For they speak not peace: but they devise deceitful matters against them that are quiet in the land.
They rob the poor because of their fine sanctuaries; they rob the poor because of their fine clothing; and they persecute the meek and the poor in heart, because in their pride they are puffed up.
Again, they are minished and brought low through oppression, affliction, and sorrow.
But chiefly them that walk after the flesh in the lust of uncleanness, and despise government. Presumptuous are they, selfwilled, they are not afraid to speak evil of dignities.
They set their mouth against the heavens, and their tongue walketh through the earth.
And they heard a great voice from heaven saying unto them, Come up hither. And they ascended up to heaven in a cloud; and their enemies beheld them.
So they shall make their own tongue to fall upon themselves: all that see them shall flee away.
And their tongues shall be stayed that they shall not utter against me; and their flesh shall fall from off their bones, and their eyes from their sockets;
But I say, Have they not heard? Yes verily, their sound went into all the earth, and their words unto the ends of the world.
Therefore his people return hither: and waters of a full cup are wrung out to them.
And he took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them: and they all drank of it.
And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink ye all of it;

Then at the drinking of this last cup, all the guests recalled some
incident of the dead, and toasts were given, one as foolish as another.
"All good for nothing. He was the only one who knew how to drink to the
dead. The departed souls must have roared with laughter when they heard
him. Sit down there, you can't come up to him." The sport ended with a
wrestling match. Two or three of the…
And it shall be, if they refuse to take the cup at thine hand to drink, then shalt thou say unto them, Thus saith the Lord of hosts; Ye shall certainly drink.

And his breath, as an overflowing stream, shall reach to the midst of the neck, to sift the nations with the sieve of vanity: and there shall be a bridle in the jaws of the people, causing them to err.
And they say, How doth God know? and is there knowledge in the most High?
Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.

Lord: for they shall all know Him even from the least unto the greatest of them.” Why? Because “they shall all be taught of God.” They looked forward to the time when “the Spirit of God shall be poured out upon all flesh;” so that all mankind shall feel the influence and be brought into union and harmony and communion with the Great God, the author of their being. That spirit will measurably rest…

especially for their interests. We all consider that if we could be taught of God it would be very well; I suppose the world generally would consider it to be a great blessing. Then the question arises in their minds, whether the teachings they receive come from God or not. How are they to know that? I know of no other way than that which is spoken in the Scriptures, “There is a spirit in man:…
And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he judge through the dark cloud?
Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain unto it.
Behold, these are the ungodly, who prosper in the world; they increase in riches.
They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;

And all their deeds manifest unrighteousness,
    And their power rests upon their riches,
Now if the fall of them be the riches of the world, and the diminishing of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?
And behold, the time cometh that he curseth your riches, that they become slippery, that ye cannot hold them; and in the days of your poverty ye cannot retain them.
Verily I have cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocency.
Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?
¶ When I would comfort myself against sorrow, my heart is faint in me.
Verily I say unto you, behold how great is your calling. Cleanse your hearts and your garments, lest the blood of this generation be required at your hands.
Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
For all the day long have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.
My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.

The next morning I found myself unable to rise from my bed,
being severely attacked with the measles.
If I say, I will speak thus; behold, I should offend against the generation of thy children.
¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
And their generations shall inherit the earth from generation to generation, forever and ever. And now I make an end of speaking unto you. Even so. Amen.

children to look pleasant we should look pleasant at them; and if we wish them to speak kind words to each other, let us speak kind words to them. We need not go into detail, but we should carry out this principle from year to year in our whole lives, and do as we wish our children to do. I say this with regard to our morals and our faith in our religion. Now let me call the attention of parents…
For behold, I say unto you that I have beheld that many generations shall pass away, and there shall be great wars and contentions among my people.
Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.
When I thought to know this, it was too painful for me;

external  sense  to  be  abundantly  occupied  with  pains,  but  it 
must  also  be  full  of  groaning.  Now  groaning  is  a  violent  and 
intense  pain.  For  we
And now, behold, when I thought this, I could remember my pains no more; yea, I was harrowed up by the memory of my sins no more.
Therefore are my loins filled with pain: pangs have taken hold upon me, as the pangs of a woman that travaileth: I was bowed down at the hearing of it; I was dismayed at the seeing of it.
Until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.

Then were assembled unto me every one that trembled at the words of the God of Israel, because of the transgression of those that had been carried away; and I sat astonied until the evening sacrifice.
Then he brought me back the way of the gate of the outward sanctuary which looketh toward the east; and it was shut.
And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.
And I heard, but I understood not: then said I, O my Lord, what shall be the end of these things?
And after I had traveled for the space of many hours in darkness, I began to pray unto the Lord that he would have mercy on me, according to the multitude of his tender mercies.
Surely thou didst set them in slippery places: thou castedst them down into destruction.
And thou didst divide the sea before them, so that they went through the midst of the sea on the dry land; and their persecutors thou threwest into the deeps, as a stone into the mighty waters.
How thou didst drive out the heathen with thy hand, and plantedst them; how thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.
Yet hast thou not walked after their ways, nor done after their abominations: but, as if that were a very little thing, thou wast corrupted more than they in all thy ways.
Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
Thy hands were not bound, nor thy feet put into fetters: as a man falleth before wicked men, so fellest thou. And all the people wept again over him.
How are they brought into desolation, as in a moment! they are utterly consumed with terrors.
Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.

But it came to pass that when they saw the danger, and the many afflictions and tribulations which the Nephites bore for them, they were moved with compassion and were desirous to take up arms in the defence of their country.
Their tents and their flocks shall they take away: they shall take to themselves their curtains, and all their vessels, and their camels; and they shall cry unto them, Fear is on every side.
As a dream when one awaketh; so, O Lord, when thou awakest, thou shalt despise their image.
Then thou scarest me with dreams, and terrifiest me through visions:
And the vision of the evening and the morning which was told is true: wherefore shut thou up the vision; for it shall be for many days.
Thy graven images also will I cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee; and thou shalt no more worship the work of thine hands.
Neither shalt thou set thee up any image; which the Lord thy God hateth.
Thy graven images I will also cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee, and thou shalt no more worship the works of thy hands;
Thus my heart was grieved, and I was pricked in my reins.
Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope:
Now therefore be not grieved, nor angry with yourselves, that ye sold me hither: for God did send me before you to preserve life.

Therefore, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts and the blindness of their minds—went forth among them in that same year, and began to testify, boldly, repentance and remission of sins through faith on the Lord Jesus Christ.
Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?
So foolish was I, and ignorant: I was as a beast before thee.
¶ Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.

¶ And I arose in the night, I and some few men with me; neither told I any man what my God had put in my heart to do at Jerusalem: neither was there any beast with me, save the beast that I rode upon.

The beast that thou sawest was, and is not; and shall ascend out of the bottomless pit, and go into perdition: and they that dwell on the earth shall wonder, whose names were not written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, when they behold the beast that was, and is not, and yet is.
O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.
Nevertheless I am continually with thee: thou hast holden me by my right hand.
By thee have I been holden up from the womb: thou art he that took me out of my mother’s bowels: my praise shall be continually of thee.
Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.

And, behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest, and will bring thee again into this land; for I will not leave thee, until I have done that which I have spoken to thee of.
And as touching the matter which thou and I have spoken of, behold, the Lord be between thee and me for ever.
The Lord judge between me and thee, and the Lord avenge me of thee: but mine hand shall not be upon thee.
Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.
Therefore, follow me, and listen to the counsel which I shall give unto you.
Keep my commandments continually, and a crown of righteousness thou shalt receive. And except thou do this, where I am you cannot come.
Be thou prepared, and prepare for thyself, thou, and all thy company that are assembled unto thee, and be thou a guard unto them.
Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.
Whom have I in heaven but thee? and there is none upon earth that I desire beside thee.
O Lord, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
Thou whom I have taken from the ends of the earth, and called thee from the chief men thereof, and said unto thee, Thou art my servant; I have chosen thee, and not cast thee away.
I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.
Wherefore thou art great, O Lord God: for there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
My flesh and my heart faileth: but God is the strength of my heart, and my portion for ever.
God is my strength and power: and he maketh my way perfect.
My heart panteth, my strength faileth me: as for the light of mine eyes, it also is gone from me.
The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in him, and I am helped: therefore my heart greatly rejoiceth; and with my song will I praise him.
In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.

¶ Mine heart within me is broken because of the prophets; all my bones shake; I am like a drunken man, and like a man whom wine hath overcome, because of the Lord, and because of the words of his holiness.
For, lo, they that are far from thee shall perish: thou hast destroyed all them that go a whoring from thee.
(For the Lord thy God is a merciful God;) he will not forsake thee, neither destroy thee, nor forget the covenant of thy fathers which he sware unto them.
But the Lord thy God shall deliver them unto thee, and shall destroy them with a mighty destruction, until they be destroyed.

For they have not served thee in their kingdom, and in thy great goodness that thou gavest them, and in the large and fat land which thou gavest before them, neither turned they from their wicked works.
And the fruits that thy soul lusted after are departed from thee, and all things which were dainty and goodly are departed from thee, and thou shalt find them no more at all.
I will do these things unto thee, because thou hast gone a whoring after the heathen, and because thou art polluted with their idols.
But it is good for me to draw near to God: I have put my trust in the Lord God, that I may declare all thy works.
But I trusted in thee, O Lord: I said, Thou art my God.
And now I come to that faith, of which I said I would speak; and I will tell you the way whereby ye may lay hold on every good thing.
Behold, this is your work, to keep my commandments, yea, with all your might, mind and strength.
O Lord my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:
